title: alstonia scholaris
intro: Alstonia scholaris, also known as the Dita bark or Milin wood, is a tropical tree native to Asia and Australia, belonging to the Apocynaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, and its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Alstonia scholaris include reducing fever, treating respiratory infections, and alleviating symptoms of anxiety and insomnia. The herbal preparations of this plant include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat various health conditions, although its efficacy and safety should be evaluated by a healthcare professional before use.
intro_study: According to the provided study, Alstonia scholaris has medicinal purposes due to the presence of hydrocarbons, triterpenes, and phytosterols.
benefits_desc: Alstonia scholaris is used to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and pneumonia. Its medicinal uses also include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and alleviating symptoms of diarrhea and dysentery. In addition to these benefits, Alstonia scholaris has been shown to lower blood pressure, reduce anxiety and stress levels, and even exhibit anti-cancer properties. Furthermore, it has been traditionally used to treat skin conditions like acne, eczema, and boils, as well as to promote wound healing and reduce the risk of infection.

actions_desc: Alstonia scholaris has been found to possess a range of therapeutic actions, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, antispasmodic, anxiolytic, sedative, and nervine properties, which can affect the nervous, digestive, and cardiovascular systems. Its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic actions can influence the musculoskeletal and integumentary systems, reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Additionally, its antispasmodic and anxiolytic properties can impact the smooth muscle system and the central nervous system, providing relief from spasms and anxiety. Furthermore, its sedative and nervine actions can affect the central nervous system, promoting relaxation and soothing the nervous system.

constituents_desc: Alstonia scholaris has been found to contain a diverse array of bioactive constituents, including flavonoids like quercetin and kaempferol, which exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is also rich in tannins, particularly gallic acid and ellagic acid, which have been shown to possess antimicrobial and anti-cancer activities. Additionally, Alstonia scholaris contains alkaloids like scholarine and vobasine, which have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects. Furthermore, the plant is a source of lignans, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, including sesquiterpenes like alstonone and alstonic acid, which have been reported to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities.

parts_desc: The most commonly used parts of Alstonia scholaris are the bark, roots, leaves, and seeds. The bark is used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, while the roots are used for their antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties to treat wounds, ulcers, and digestive issues. The leaves are used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, and are also used to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. The seeds are used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, and are also used as a diuretic to treat urinary tract infections and kidney stones.

preparations_desc: Alstonia scholaris most common herbal preparations are infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, which are used for various health purposes. Infusions of Alstonia scholaris are commonly used to treat fever, sore throats, and coughs, and are also taken as a natural remedy for anxiety and insomnia, while its tinctures are often used to relieve symptoms of indigestion and constipation. Decoctions made from the bark and roots of the plant are employed to treat infections, wounds, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne, and are also used to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Additionally, Alstonia scholaris preparations are used to alleviate symptoms of menopausal hot flashes, menstrual cramps, and other gynecological disorders, making it a versatile herbal remedy for various health issues.

side_effects_desc: Alstonia scholaris side effects are primarily observed in individuals who consume the plant's extract or use its products in excessive quantities. Common side effects may include dizziness, headaches, nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, stomach pain, and allergic reactions such as rashes and itching. The elderly and individuals with pre-existing liver or kidney conditions are most likely to experience these side effects due to their compromised bodily functions. Precautions include avoiding Alstonia scholaris consumption during pregnancy, breastfeeding, or in combination with other medications, as it may exacerbate side effects like fatigue, insomnia, and tremors.
